title: lappula fruticulosa
intro: Lappula fruticulosa, also known as the annual delphinium, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, Lappula fruticulosa is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are typically consumed as teas or added to other herbal remedies.
